body_excerpt: {"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/29173071?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/NSSYS", "id": 29173071, "login": "NSSYS", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTczMDcx", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/NSSYS", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-45306", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-706", "name": "Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference"}], "description": "## Summary The fix for CVE-2026-33509 prevents setting `storage_folder` inside `PKGDIR` or `userdir`, but does NOT protect the Flask session directory (`/tmp/pyLoad/flask`). An authenticated attacker can set `storage_folder` to the session directory and download session files of other users via `/files/get/`, leading to account takeover. ## Details The fix in `src/pyload/core/api/__init__.py`: ```python directories = [PKGDIR, userdir] if any(directories[0].startswith(d) for d in directories[1:]): return # blocked ``` But the Flask session directory is: ```python session_storage_path = os.path.join(api.get_cachedir(), "flask") # = /tmp/pyLoad/flask ← NOT blocked by fix ``` ## Attack Chain 1. Attacker (admin) sets `storage_folder = /tmp/pyLoad/flask` 2. Fix does NOT block this — `/tmp/pyLoad/flask` not inside `PKGDIR` or `userdir` 3. Attacker requests `GET /files/get/` 4. `send_from_directory('/tmp/pyLoad/flask', session_file)` serves victim's session 5. Attacker uses stolen session → **Account Takeover** ## PoC ```python import os PKGDIR = "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/pyload" userdir = os.path.expanduser("~/.pyload") session_dir = "/tmp/pyLoad/flask" correct_case = lambda x: x directories = [ correct_case(os.path.join(os.path.realpath(d), "")) for d in [session_dir, PKGDIR, userdir] ] blocked = any(directories[0].startswith(d) for d in directories[1:]) print(f"Fix blocks session_dir: {blocked}") # Output: Fix blocks session_dir: False ← BYPASS CONFIRMED ``` ## Impact Authenticated admin can steal sessions of other users → Account Takeover. ## Suggested Fix ```python blocked_dirs = [PKGDIR, userdir, api.get_cachedir()] directories = [ os.path.join(os.path.realpath(d), "") for d in [value] + blocked_dirs ] if any(directories[0].startswith(d) for d in directories[1:]): return ```", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00234, "percentile": 0.14079}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-w727-595x-pc3r", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-14T20:17:27Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w727-595x-pc3r", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-w727-595x-pc3r"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-45306"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-28T18:16:34Z", "published_at": "2026-05-14T20:17:27Z", "references": ["https://github.com/pyload/pyload/security/advisories/GHSA-w727-595x-pc3r", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-r7mc-x6x7-cqxx", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-45306", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w727-595x-pc3r"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/pyload/pyload/security-advisories/GHSA-w727-595x-pc3r", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/pyload/pyload", "summary": "pyLoad Has Incomplete Fix for CVE-2026-33509 -storage_folder Bypass via Session Directory in pyLoad", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T10:19:37Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-w727-595x-pc3r", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "pyload-ng"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.5.0b3.dev99"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: pyLoad Has Incomplete Fix for CVE-2026-33509 -storage_folder Bypass via Session Directory in pyLoad
